summary: Crisp FM broadcast captures a stellar March 30 1990 show highlighted by a goose‑bumps “Help on the Way” opener, a smoking Help>Slip>Franklin’s sequence and the rare encore “Attics”.
review: The recording is an FM broadcast with clear, radio‑level mix that lets every nuance shine. The performance is praised for its high energy, standout sequences (Help → Slip → Franklin’s) and a rare Attics encore, with several fans calling it one of the best from the spring‑1990 tour.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers are enthusiastic, give 4‑5 stars, and highlight both superb musical moments and excellent FM broadcast sound quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FM broadcast provides a clean, balanced mix; reviewers describe it as “great live sound”, “crisp” and capable of giving goose‑bumps from the first note.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note an energetic first set, a flowing second set with the signature Help>Slip>Franklin’s sequence, and a rare Attics encore that caps off a consistently inspired performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Jerry’s guitar work is highlighted as a goose‑bumps trigger from the opening of Help on the Way; reviewers thank him directly.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s playing is described as “inspired” and a key factor in the band’s elevated energy during this stretch of shows.
